question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: aggregateHbarTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by crafting a transaction where `itemTransfer` contains millions of entries with the same `EntityId.EncodedId`, causing `transferMap[transfer.EntityId.EncodedId] += transfer.Amount` to perform millions of map updates on a single key, consuming CPU without growing memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFeeHbarTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by crafting a transaction where `cryptoTransfers` contains millions of entries with the same `AccountId.EncodedId`, causing the `cryptoTransferMap[accountId] = hbarTransfer{Amount: transfer.Amount + cryptoTransferMap[accountId].Amount}` accumulation to perform millions of map reads and writes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: FindBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the fact that `make([]*transaction, 0)` for `transactionsBatch` does not pre-allocate capacity, causing repeated slice growth and copying as the batch fills up to 2000 entries, multiplied across many batches?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: constructTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by crafting a transaction where `Memo []byte` is extremely large (e.g., megabytes), causing `result.Memo = firstTransaction.Memo` to hold a large byte slice in memory for every transaction in the result set?
